comment: Quality Gates catalog — release dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/release/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/release/.

asserts: the response body is >= 1024 bytes and starts with '#!'

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release.yml did not fire on the last reposix-cli-v* tag (per P56 RELEASE-01 root cause), or release-plz cut a non-cli per-crate release after the cli release moving the latest pointer (P56 SURPRISES.md row 1)

if RED: release.yml did not fire on the last reposix-cli-v* tag (per P56 RELEASE-01 root cause), or release-plz cut a non-cli per-crate release after the cli release moving the latest pointer (P56 SURPRISES.md row 1)

asserts: window-aware: the formula's `version "X.Y.Z"` equals the crates.io reposix-cli max_version in steady state, OR (during an in-flight release window) the crates.io max_version equals the [workspace.package] version in Cargo.toml AND the formula version equals the latest published GitHub release tag (leading `v` stripped) -- so a just-merged release-plz bump that publishes to crates.io before the v-tag is pushed does not false-RED this gate

rows.owner_hint: if RED: .github/workflows/release.yml upload-homebrew-formula job did not fire (P56 root cause was missing tag glob), or HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret expired

rows.command: git clone https://github.com/reubenjohn/reposix && cd reposix && cargo build --release --workspace --bins

asserts: the most recent successful run of ci.yml on main branch's `test` job exited 0 (build-from-source is covered by the workspace cargo test build)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: ci.yml `test` job is failing on main; investigate ci.yml workflow logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-cli at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-remote at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-core at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-cache at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-sim at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-github at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-confluence at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `crate.max_version` field equals the workspace.package.version in Cargo.toml (e.g., '0.11.3')

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release-plz failed to publish reposix-jira at the workspace version; check .github/workflows/release-plz.yml run logs

asserts: the response's `assets` array has length >= 7 (5 platform archives + 2 installers; SHA256SUMS is bonus)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release.yml did not fire on the last reposix-cli-v* tag (P56 SURPRISES.md row 2: GITHUB_TOKEN-tag-push exemption; recovery via `gh workflow run release.yml --ref reposix-cli-vX.Y.Z`)

asserts: window-aware: decoding the base64 `content` field and parsing for `version "X.Y.Z"` yields a string that equals the crates.io reposix-cli max_version in steady state, OR (during an in-flight release window) the crates.io max_version equals the [workspace.package] version in Cargo.toml AND the formula version equals the latest published GitHub release tag (leading `v` stripped) -- release-plz publishes the bump to crates.io before the v-tag is pushed, so this window is legitimate and must PASS, while a formula stale relative to a COMPLETED release still FAILs

rows.owner_hint: if RED: release.yml upload-homebrew-formula job did not run (HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN missing or expired), or release-plz published a per-crate release between cli releases (P56 SURPRISES.md row 1 latest-pointer drift)

rows._provenance_note: P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): the historical PARTIAL-acceptable carve-out (P56 SURPRISES.md row 3) is RETIRED -- the pkg-url metadata fix landed pre-P90 (commit 33dd41f, QL-003); PASS is now the expected outcome on the next real container run, not PARTIAL. || post-v0.13.0 (2026-07-07, CI run 28839335746): PASS logic broadened from a single literal-URL substring match to a set of accepted vendor wordings, because cargo-binstall's own stdout wording drifted between versions and false-negatived a genuinely-successful resolve (rc=0, prebuilt binary resolved in ~1.97s). Contract is now invariant-shaped (resolved + will-install-binaries line), not surface-string-shaped; see REGRESSION NOTE in quality/gates/release/cargo-binstall-resolves.py

asserts: in a fresh rust:1.82-slim container with cargo-binstall installed, `cargo binstall --no-confirm --dry-run reposix-cli` resolves to a prebuilt GitHub Release binary: rc==0 AND a 'downloaded from github' signal is present (accepts EITHER the legacy 'github.com/reubenjohn/reposix/releases/download/' URL echo OR cargo-binstall's newer 'has been downloaded from github.com' summary wording, case-insensitive) AND the 'will install the following binaries' line is present

rows.owner_hint: P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): WAIVER CLEARED per D90-02(a) -- LANDED, not renewed. The ~10-LOC pkg-url alignment this row's waiver named was ALREADY SHIPPED pre-P90 by commit 33dd41f 'fix(binstall): correct pkg-url to real release asset name (QL-003)' (2026-07-03, an ancestor of P90's HEAD before 90-05 started -- D90-11-style already-fixed disposition, not re-done here). Verified against reality in THIS dispatch by re-deriving both templates against release.yml's actual archive-staging logic (release.yml:167-193: STAGE="reposix-${version_tag}-${target}", archive="${STAGE}.tar.gz" or .zip) and confirming byte-for-byte match against crates/reposix-cli/Cargo.toml + crates/reposix-remote/Cargo.toml's current [package.metadata.binstall] pkg-url/bin-dir templates (both crates identical shape). If RED: the binstall metadata regressed again OR release.yml's archive-staging logic changed without a matching Cargo.toml update — check release.yml AND crates/{reposix-cli,reposix-remote}/Cargo.toml [package.metadata.binstall].
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the pkg-url metadata actually still mismatched release.yml's archive shape, a real `cargo binstall --dry-run reposix-cli` in a fresh container would report a 404 or a 'falling back to source' signal instead of resolving the prebuilt binary URL -- that observable failure is what would falsify this row's PASS claim, not the manual template comparison alone.
